questions: Which of the three named capabilities actually exist as absorbable code?
What are the migration tool mechanics and its gaps vs house discipline?
What is the honest slicing for #252?

findings.claim: Only the migration tool exists to absorb; mirroring is design-only upstream (5 unresolved operator decisions, explicit execution gate); the SQLite adapter is already distributed via bicameral-mcp

13 patterns over 3 legacy formats; extract->strip->inject canonical; never in-place; dual-formula chain validation reporting not rejecting; gaps: no dry-run, no idempotence guarantee, exit 0 unconditional

Our own verify reports 32 entries skipped for non-verifiable markup (legacy variance the read-side already tolerates via legacy_chain_hash); migration makes them verifiable, applied only by operator decision
Phase 170: port as ledger_migrate with house discipline + document the two deferred thirds with named owners; #252 closes with the corrected premise recorded
